doc_title: Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews — PRISMA 2020 Statement
label: partial
reason: The real PRISMA 2020 citation and general description are accurate, but the "Citation Verification" and "Common Errors in Citation Practice" sections are fabricated content not present in the actual PRISMA 2020 statement.
response_turn1:
LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 92
REASON: The citation (Page et al., BMJ 2021, doi:10.1136/bmj.n71) and general framing are genuine, but the sections on "Citation Verification" and "Common Errors in Citation Practice" are fabricated additions not found in the actual PRISMA 2020 statement, which contains a 27-item checklist focused on reporting transparency rather than citation-policing obligations or research misconduct definitions.
